target: data/sections.json part2b SEC-2b.7 — verify ISS-802 concerns resolved in current text
summary: Both concerns raised in ISS-802 (causal inference overstated; scoring criterion needs citation) are already resolved in the live SEC-2b.7 prose in data/sections.json. No further text patches required. Recommend closing ISS-802.

concern_1_causal_inference.issue_text: Paragraph states scoreboard was removed approximately one day after our poller flagged its drift — this conflates correlation with causation. Separate factual claim (endpoint returns 404) from causal inference.
concern_1_causal_inference.evidence: The current closing paragraph now reads: 'Whether this removal was prompted by our analysis or by an independent API change, the effect is the same: the structured scoreboard that showed 100% by construction is no longer available for independent verification.' Followed by the four-anomaly recap and the 'reproduced across independent fetches on 2026-04-09 and 2026-04-10' framing. The original overreach ('the endpoint was taken down shortly after this review documented its scoring pathologies') is gone. Timing is given precisely ('confirmed live at 2026-04-10T18:12 UTC ... returned 404 from all tested URL variants by 2026-04-11T07:14 UTC — a removal window of approximately 13 hours'). This matches the curmudgeon recommendation in SEC-2b.7-rewrite.c2.json, hole 1.

concern_2_scoring_criterion_citation.issue_text: The direction_correct AND snr_sufficient scoring criterion is stated as fact without citation from the dome code.
concern_2_scoring_criterion_citation.evidence: The current text reads: 'results.json defines a pass as direction_correct AND snr_sufficient (field names confirmed by independent re-fetch in project record ISS-705-verification.json), without requiring the observation to lie inside the pre-registered uncertainty band.' Citation is explicit and traceable to our project record ISS-705-verification.json, which independently re-fetched results.json on 2026-04-10 and dumped the actual field names for W001, W004, and TASK-3-1. This matches the curmudgeon recommendation in SEC-2b.7-rewrite.c2.json, hole 2.
concern_2_scoring_criterion_citation.location: data/sections.json part2b.html — paragraph beginning 'The mechanism of the contradiction is revealing.'

related_c2_issues_also_resolved.evidence: Current text uses full disambiguation: 'results.json entry W004 (the dome internal API label for the 2024 Eclipse 9-Station Replication — corresponding to our WIN-025; not to be confused with wins-page WIN-004 which refers to Noise Floor Limit)' — matches the ISS-705-finding recommendation.
Timing tightened to UTC timestamps with 13-hour window — matches curmudgeon recommendation.

Text now says: 'We examined results.json for overshoot or outlier thresholds and found none — the scoring fields contain no upper bound on acceptable deviation in the direction-correct case; any negative observation, no matter how far from the predicted value, receives a pass.'

Replaced with the four-anomaly recap language recommended by curmudgeon: 'The four scoring anomalies documented above — scoreboard split, pred=obs hardcoding, null-vs-null confirmation, and detection-threshold exclusion — were properties of the scoring rules as expressed in the live JSON, not transient data-entry errors. Each anomaly was reproduced across independent fetches on 2026-04-09 and 2026-04-10. The removal of the endpoint does not retroactively change what the rules computed while they were live.'

Text now explicitly says 'when the observed value came in at +3.73 nT (wrong sign; sigma_distance 7.3 relative to the ≈0.8 nT prediction uncertainty; direction_correct: false)' and calls out the compute-but-not-count inconsistency — the stronger framing from stronger_arguments[1] in the curmudgeon review.

Finlay et al. citation no longer appears in the section. The generic argument (precision-to-capability gap quantified as ~0.8 nT vs instrument noise floor) is now used instead, matching the curmudgeon recommendation to replace citation-to-quantity extrapolation with the general argument.

still_open_minor_items.note: Bold 'Kernel of truth.' label was called out as stylistically inconsistent with 2b.1-2b.6 which do not use that label. Current 2b.7 text does not use a bold 'Kernel of truth.' label either — appears already resolved or never applied. Not a blocker for closing ISS-802.

github_routing_investigation.description: Investigated whether the 2026-04-11 404 on /api/current/results.json represents a deliberate editorial removal vs. a GitHub Pages routing/deployment issue. Checked dome commit history in the removal window (2026-04-10T18:00Z → 2026-04-11T08:00Z) via GitHub API: 16 commits, all automated 'Auto-update ECM status' or 'auto: ECM data pull' messages. None of them modified api/current/results.json. Direct check of the file today (2026-04-14) shows: raw.githubusercontent.com/john09289/predictions/main/api/current/results.json returns HTTP 200 with a 35-entry array still present. GitHub Pages endpoint https://john09289.github.io/predictions/api/current/results.json returns HTTP 404.
github_routing_investigation.implication: The 404 is almost certainly a GitHub Pages build or routing artifact — the static file still exists in the main branch and is served by raw.githubusercontent.com. The dome author did not commit a deletion. This is consistent with — and strengthens — the current text deliberately neutral framing ('whether prompted by our analysis or by an independent API change'). The previous causal framing would have been not just methodologically overreaching but factually wrong.
github_routing_investigation.recommendation_optional: OPTIONAL future edit (not required for ISS-802 closure): if the decider wants to preempt a dome defender saying 'we never removed it, GitHub Pages just lost the route,' add a parenthetical to the endpoint-removed paragraph noting that the raw-blob URL still serves the file, so the analysis can still be verified against the preserved data. The current neutral framing already accommodates this; this would only sharpen it.

kernel_of_truth: Both ISS-802 concerns represent the same intellectual discipline we hold the dome to — separating facts from causal inferences, and citing every non-trivial claim. The curmudgeon was right to flag them, and the patch that addressed them strengthened the section rather than weakening it. Closing ISS-802 without a new patch is correct because the patch has already landed.
tldr_evidence: Both ISS-802 concerns are verifiably resolved in the current SEC-2b.7 prose: the causal-inference overreach is replaced with neutral 'whether prompted by our analysis or by an independent API change' framing, and the scoring criterion now cites project record ISS-705-verification.json. Several related c2 curmudgeon issues are also resolved in the same rewrite.
tldr_verdict: ISS-802 can be closed. No patch needed. Supplementary finding: the 2026-04-11 404 appears to be a GitHub Pages routing issue rather than an editorial removal — the file still lives in the main branch — which strengthens rather than weakens the current neutral framing.
